This article details [[Toulouse Olympique]] rugby league football club's 2016 season. This was Toulouse's first season back in the English competition since they left at the end of 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34515899 |title=Toulouse: RFL confirm French club for League One from 2016 |work=BBC Sport |date=13 October 2015 |access-date=4 May 2026}}</ref> They competed in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. They finished the season on top of the table, but lost the promotion final to [[Rochdale Hornets]] who were crowned champions. Toulouse were promoted to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] in second place after they successfully came through the play-offs, beating [[Barrow Raiders]] in the play-off final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/expansionist-blog-toulouse-olympique-unlucky-not-super-league |title=Expansionist Blog: Toulouse Olympique are so unlucky not to be in Super League |website=Love Rugby League |date=9 November 2017 |access-date=4 May 2026}}</ref> Toulouse also entered the Challenge Cup, reaching the sixth round where they lost to [[Super League]] side Wakefield Trinity.

Home matches were played at [[Stade Ernest-Argelès]] in [[Blagnac]] rather than at Toulouse's historic home ground of [[Stade des Minimes|Stade Arnauné]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-to-play-at-blagnac-in-2016 |title=Toulouse to play at Blagnac in 2016 |website=Love Rugby League |date=22 January 2016 |access-date=4 May 2026}}</ref>

===Challenge Cup===
{{main|2016 Challenge Cup}}
Toulouse entered the [[2016 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] in Round 3. They were drawn away to face Cumbrian amateur side [[Wath Brow Hornets]], winning 32–14. In Round 4, Toulouse defeated fellow League 1 side [[Gloucestershire All Golds]] 62–28 in France. In Round 5 they beat [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Leigh Centurions]] 10–8 at [[Stade Ernest-Argeles]] before bowing out at [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]], losing 40–22.
